extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3, whose condition C is the 8-way conjunction (qt_prolongation AND g6pd_deficiency AND peptic_ulcer AND ckd_dialysis AND low_ejection_fraction AND polypharmacy AND heart_failure AND seizure_history). Thus rec_drug_a=true requires C to hold; otherwise it defaults false. For rec_drug_b, two rules can fire: r5 [priority 50] sets it true when qt_prolongation, and r6 [priority 90] sets it false when C holds. Whenever rec_drug_a=true, C necessarily holds, so both r5 and r6 match for rec_drug_b; since per-output the highest-priority matching rule wins and r6 (90) outranks r5 (50), r6 forces rec_drug_b:=false. Therefore (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is unsatisfiable over the entire input space. r3 is the sole writer of rec_drug_a, and r5/r6 are the only writers of rec_drug_b, so no other interaction exists. The property holds.
